abstract: Azabicyclo[2.1.1]hexanes (aza-BCHs) and bicyclo[1.1.1]pentanes (BCPs) represent a crucial class of *sp*³-rich motifs, frequently employed as three-dimensional bioisosteres to replace flat aromatic rings and enhance metabolic stability in drug candidates. Despite their utility, methods to directly interconvert or "scaffold hop" between these frameworks via single-atom skeletal editing remain scarce. Here, we report a strategy to bridge these two chemical spaces through a formal nitrogen-deletion process. By coupling photochemical [2+2] cycloadditions—which provide rapid access to multifunctionalized aza-BCHs—with a subsequent deamination step, we demonstrate the synthesis of bridge-functionalized BCPs. This approach addresses a significant synthetic gap, as BCPs with complex bridge substitution are typically difficult to access. This modular sequence offers a versatile pathway to diverse, privileged bicyclic scaffolds of high pharmaceutical relevance.
